message:
chore(beads): sync issue tracker state and add dependency wiring script

.beads/issues.jsonl:
- Updated issue tracker reflecting latest state after ARS intercept,
  trauma guard, and UBS-derived epic creation

fix_deps.sh:
- Utility script that wires parent-child dependency relationships
  between the UBS-derived epics and their child tasks using br CLI
- Looks up bead IDs by title via br list --json, then calls br dep add
  to establish the dependency graph across all 4 epics (Panic Eradication,
  Concurrency Safety, Memory Safety, Robustness/Performance)
- Idempotent: safe to re-run if dependencies are already wired

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"ft-2p9cb.3.4","title":"[AARSP][C4] Adaptive transport policy and compression bypass control","description":"## Objective
Implement adaptive local/remote transport policy including compression bypass where beneficial.

## Acceptance Criteria
1. Policy selects transport mode based on measurable cost model.
2. Local fast-path avoids unnecessary compression overhead.
3. Unit + e2e tests validate correctness and mode-selection stability.
4. Logs include mode decisions, savings, and fallback reasons.

## User Impact
Lower local latency and stable remote performance.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-23T00:13:06.629826Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-02-23T00:18:30.305320Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["alien-artifact","determinism","e2e-tests","extreme-optimization","performance","quality-gate","reactivity","reliability","test-coverage","test-logging","transport","user-impact"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ft-2p9cb.3.4","depends_on_id":"ft-2p9cb.3","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-23T00:14:27Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"ft-2p9cb.3.4","depends_on_id":"ft-2p9cb.3.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-23T00:14:27Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":1201,"issue_id":"ft-2p9cb.3.4","author":"Jeff Emanuel","text":"Execution split added for implementation rigor and testability:\n1. ft-2p9cb.3.4.1 (spec/contracts/benchmark/test matrix)\n2. ft-2p9cb.3.4.2 (deterministic implementation + guardrails)\n3. ft-2p9cb.3.4.3 (unit/property/integration/e2e + evidence/CI gates)\n\nDependency chain: ft-2p9cb.3.4.2 -> ft-2p9cb.3.4.1 and ft-2p9cb.3.4.3 -> ft-2p9cb.3.4.2.\nParent-child linkage keeps the subtask relationship explicit while preserving DAG integrity.","created_at":"2026-02-23T00:18:30Z"}]}

 {"id":"ft-2p9cb.3.5","title":"[AARSP][C5] Kernel/hardware tail-latency eradication lane (syscall/wakeup/NUMA/affinity)","description":"## Background
Imported overlap from retired tracks (`ft-ocplf.9*`, `ft-189at.2.6`, portions of `ft-1u90p.10.8*`) requires explicit treatment of kernel/hardware pathologies (syscall batching, wakeup/timer precision, NUMA/affinity, cache coherence) inside the canonical reactivity tree.

## Objective
Guarantee tail-latency resilience on interaction-critical paths by making kernel/hardware behavior an explicit, test-gated lane.

## Scope
1. Syscall/wakeup/timer path attribution and optimization contract.
2. NUMA/cache/affinity placement policy for hot paths.
3. Tail-risk guardrails and rollback criteria for hardware-sensitive optimizations.

## Acceptance Criteria
1. Dedicated unit/property/concurrency tests exist for scheduling, batching, wakeup, and affinity invariants.
2. Deterministic e2e stress scripts validate p95/p99/p999 behavior under CPU pressure, timer jitter, and mixed I/O patterns.
3. Structured evidence artifacts include latency stage attribution, reason codes, and rollback trigger metrics.
4. CI promotion fails if kernel/hardware optimization regresses user-perceived responsiveness or determinism.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-23T04:57:23.383988Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-02-23T04:57:25.469121Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["hardware","kernel","performance","reactivity","test-coverage","test-logging"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ft-2p9cb.3.5","depends_on_id":"ft-1u90p.9.1","type":"related","created_at":"2026-02-23T04:57:25.469072Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"ft-2p9cb.3.5","depends_on_id":"ft-2p9cb.3","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-23T04:57:23.383988Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"ft-2p9cb.3.5","depends_on_id":"ft-e34d9.10.4","type":"related","created_at":"2026-02-23T04:57:25.167353Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

 {"id":"ft-tkpgy.3.3","title":"[ARS][T3.3] Dynamic YAML Compilation & Injection (Snapshot/WaitFor)","description":"## Background & Justification
Compile the templated command, the timeout, and the constraints into a `WorkflowDescriptor`.

## Considerations
Must auto-inject a `DescriptorStep::SnapshotTerminal` before the action, and a `DescriptorStep::WaitFor` (or check exit code) after.
Also handles the **Operator Override Gate**: abort compilation if an operator has locked a manual YAML for this cluster.

## Acceptance Criteria
1. Scope is fully preserved with no feature/behavior regression relative to the parent contract.
2. Comprehensive unit tests cover happy paths, boundary/edge cases, and explicit failure/error branches.
3. Deterministic integration and end-to-end scripts validate nominal, degraded, failure, and recovery scenarios.
4. Validation emits structured logs/artifacts with at least: `timestamp`, `component`, `scenario_id`, `correlation_id`, `decision_path`, `inputs`, `outcome`, `reason_code`/`error_code`, and `artifact_path`.
5. Release/promotion decisions are gated by auditable evidence bundles and explicit go/no-go criteria.","acceptance_criteria":"1. Emits schema-valid `WorkflowDescriptor` YAML containing the injected pre/post steps.
2. Honors the operator override lock, cleanly aborting synthesis and logging the bypass.
3. Variables mapped via Jinja match the generalized parameters from T2.4.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-23T00:14:32.656975Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-02-23T22:02:56.094446Z","closed_at":"2026-02-23T22:02:56.094425Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ft-tkpgy.3.3","depends_on_id":"ft-tkpgy.3","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-23T00:14:32.656975Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"ft-tkpgy.3.3","depends_on_id":"ft-tkpgy.3.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-23T00:14:32.656975Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"ft-tkpgy.3.4","title":"[ARS][T3.4] O(1) Finite State Transducer (FST) Compiler for Reflex Signatures","description":"## Background & Justification
As the ARS database grows to millions of reflexes across the federation, linear or hash-map lookups might stall.
Compiling the MinHash signatures and trigger contexts into a Finite State Transducer (FST) guarantees $O(K)$ lookup time where $K$ is the length of the query, utterly independent of the total number of stored reflexes.

## Considerations
Whenever a new reflex is promoted to Active, the background compiler thread rebuilds the FST and hot-swaps it atomically via an `ArcSwap`.

## Acceptance Criteria
1. Scope is fully preserved with no feature/behavior regression relative to the parent contract.
2. Comprehensive unit tests cover happy paths, boundary/edge cases, and explicit failure/error branches.
3. Deterministic integration and end-to-end scripts validate nominal, degraded, failure, and recovery scenarios.
4. Validation emits structured logs/artifacts with at least: `timestamp`, `component`, `scenario_id`, `correlation_id`, `decision_path`, `inputs`, `outcome`, `reason_code`/`error_code`, and `artifact_path`.
5. Release/promotion decisions are gated by auditable evidence bundles and explicit go/no-go criteria.","acceptance_criteria":"1. `cargo bench` proves lookup time is bounded by $O(K)$ and unaffected by scaling the reflex cache from 10 to 1,000,000 entries.
2. Background thread successfully rebuilds and atomically swaps the FST without locking readers.
3. Correctly maps MinHash queries to their respective `WorkflowDescriptor` IDs.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-23T00:14:32.706260Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-02-23T22:08:11.451856Z","closed_at":"2026-02-23T22:08:11.451842Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ft-tkpgy.3.4","depends_on_id":"ft-tkpgy.3","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-23T00:14:32.706260Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"ft-tkpgy.3.4","depends_on_id":"ft-tkpgy.3.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-23T00:14:32.706260Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"ft-tkpgy.4","title":"[ARS] Execution: O(1) Interception & Conformal Drift Detection","description":"## Objective
Execute reflexes at microsecond latency while maintaining strict blast radius control and anytime-valid semantic drift detection.

## Subtasks
- **O(1) Interception:** Wire the FST evaluation into the hot path. If an error matches, halt LLM dispatch immediately.
- **Conformal Drift Detection (e-values):** Replace arbitrary moving averages. Use e-values to perform continuous, anytime-valid testing of the reflex's success rate against a null hypothesis of degradation. If the e-value exceeds $1/lpha$ (e.g., $lpha = 0.01$), formally declare semantic drift and demote the reflex, strictly bounding the false-alarm rate.
- **Reflex Evolution:** If demoted, let the agent resolve the drifted error. Synthesize `v2` via MDL, deprecate `v1`, and record the transition in the Evidence Ledger.
- **Blast Radius Control:** Enforce a token-bucket rate limit on reflex concurrency across the swarm to contain unknown unknowns.
- **Context Evaluation:** Evaluate the target pane's CWD/Env against the reflex's required context bounds.

## Considerations & Future-Proofing
By using e-values, the system never demotes a reflex due to "bad luck" (statistical noise). It only demotes when there is formal mathematical proof that the underlying environment has shifted (Semantic Drift).

## Acceptance Criteria
1. Scope is fully preserved with no feature/behavior regression relative to the parent contract.
2. Comprehensive unit tests cover happy paths, boundary/edge cases, and explicit failure/error branches.
3. Deterministic integration and end-to-end scripts validate nominal, degraded, failure, and recovery scenarios.
4. Validation emits structured logs/artifacts with at least: `timestamp`, `component`, `scenario_id`, `correlation_id`, `decision_path`, `inputs`, `outcome`, `reason_code`/`error_code`, and `artifact_path`.
5. Release/promotion decisions are gated by auditable evidence bundles and explicit go/no-go criteria.","acceptance_criteria":"1. **Anytime-Valid Demotion:** Unit tests prove the e-value drift detector correctly bounds false-positive demotions to exactly the configured $lpha$ level over 10,000 simulated executions.
2. **Evolution:** Integration tests verify a demoted `v1` reflex is correctly superseded by a newly synthesized `v2` reflex when the agent discovers the updated fix.
3. **Blast Radius:** Token-bucket rate limiting strictly prevents swarm-wide cascading executions in simulated stress tests.
4. **Context Gates:** The reflex is hard-rejected if the target pane's environment variables violate the compiled context preconditions.","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-02-22T22:44:45.022751Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-02-23T19:10:22.379217Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["e2e-tests","integration-tests","logging","quality-gate","test-coverage","test-logging","user-impact"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ft-tkpgy.4","depends_on_id":"ft-tkpgy","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-23T00:14:28Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"ft-tkpgy.4","depends_on_id":"ft-tkpgy.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-23T00:14:28Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
-{"id":"ft-tkpgy.4.1","title":"[ARS][T4.1] FST Interception & PTY Hot-Path Integration","description":"## Background & Justification
Wire the compiled FST directly into the PTY event loop. When BOCPD identifies an error regime and generates a MinHash, query the FST.

## Considerations
If a match is found, immediately halt the standard LLM dispatch pipeline. We are entering the subconscious execution mode.
Evaluate the Context Gate (do the target pane's CWD/Env match the reflex's required bounds?). If context fails, gracefully fall back to LLM.

## Acceptance Criteria
1. Scope is fully preserved with no feature/behavior regression relative to the parent contract.
2. Comprehensive unit tests cover happy paths, boundary/edge cases, and explicit failure/error branches.
3. Deterministic integration and end-to-end scripts validate nominal, degraded, failure, and recovery scenarios.
4. Validation emits structured logs/artifacts with at least: `timestamp`, `component`, `scenario_id`, `correlation_id`, `decision_path`, `inputs`, `outcome`, `reason_code`/`error_code`, and `artifact_path`.
5. Release/promotion decisions are gated by auditable evidence bundles and explicit go/no-go criteria.","acceptance_criteria":"1. Integration tests prove standard LLM dispatch is bypassed when the FST registers a hit.
2. Context Gate successfully blocks execution and falls back if the pane's CWD differs from the reflex requirements.
3. Hot-path overhead remains < 1ms.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-23T00:14:32.757897Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-02-23T19:10:26.161332Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ft-tkpgy.4.1","depends_on_id":"ft-tkpgy.3.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-23T00:14:32.757897Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"ft-tkpgy.4.1","depends_on_id":"ft-tkpgy.4","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-23T00:14:32.757897Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

 {"id":"ft-tkpgy.4.2","title":"[ARS][T4.2] Swarm-Wide Token-Bucket Blast Radius Controller","description":"## Background & Justification
If a reflex is subtly wrong, it must not destroy 200 agents simultaneously. We need swarm-wide concurrency limits.

## Considerations
Implement a distributed Token-Bucket rate limiter (or local shard equivalent). A newly learned reflex might be capped at `5 executions / minute / swarm`. Excess requests fall back to LLM.

## Acceptance Criteria
1. Scope is fully preserved with no feature/behavior regression relative to the parent contract.
2. Comprehensive unit tests cover happy paths, boundary/edge cases, and explicit failure/error branches.
3. Deterministic integration and end-to-end scripts validate nominal, degraded, failure, and recovery scenarios.
4. Validation emits structured logs/artifacts with at least: `timestamp`, `component`, `scenario_id`, `correlation_id`, `decision_path`, `inputs`, `outcome`, `reason_code`/`error_code`, and `artifact_path`.
5. Release/promotion decisions are gated by auditable evidence bundles and explicit go/no-go criteria.","acceptance_criteria":"1. Rate limiter accurately caps concurrency according to configured limits.

 {"id":"ft-tkpgy.4.3","title":"[ARS][T4.3] Anytime-Valid Conformal Drift Detection (e-values)","description":"## Background & Justification
Instead of arbitrary moving averages for success rate, use formal e-values (anytime-valid testing) to detect semantic drift (e.g., underlying CLI updated, reflex now fails).

## Considerations
The e-value formally bounds the false-alarm rate $lpha$. If the e-value exceeds $1/lpha$, we have mathematical proof the reflex has drifted. The reflex is instantly demoted to Shadow Mode.

## Acceptance Criteria
1. Scope is fully preserved with no feature/behavior regression relative to the parent contract.
2. Comprehensive unit tests cover happy paths, boundary/edge cases, and explicit failure/error branches.
3. Deterministic integration and end-to-end scripts validate nominal, degraded, failure, and recovery scenarios.
4. Validation emits structured logs/artifacts with at least: `timestamp`, `component`, `scenario_id`, `correlation_id`, `decision_path`, `inputs`, `outcome`, `reason_code`/`error_code`, and `artifact_path`.
5. Release/promotion decisions are gated by auditable evidence bundles and explicit go/no-go criteria.","acceptance_criteria":"1. Unit tests prove the e-value correctly bounds false-positive demotions to the configured $lpha$ level over 10,000 simulations.
2. Automatically demotes reflexes and emits a high-priority warning log when semantic drift is proven.
3. Integrates the e-value tracking state into persistent storage.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-23T00:14:32.911423Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-02-23T19:10:25.473709Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ft-tkpgy.4.3","depends_on_id":"ft-tkpgy.4","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-23T00:14:32.911423Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"ft-tkpgy.4.3","depends_on_id":"ft-tkpgy.4.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-23T00:14:32.911423Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"ft-tkpgy.4.4","title":"[ARS][T4.4] Reflex Evolution Engine (v1 -> v2 synthesis mapping)","description":"## Background & Justification
When a reflex is demoted via T4.3, the agent resolves the new error via LLM. The ARS must recognize this as an evolution.

## Considerations
Synthesize the new sequence, tag it as `ars_version: 2`, map its parent to `v1`, and promote it. Deprecate `v1` completely. This allows the immune system to continuously adapt without human intervention.

## Acceptance Criteria
1. Scope is fully preserved with no feature/behavior regression relative to the parent contract.
2. Comprehensive unit tests cover happy paths, boundary/edge cases, and explicit failure/error branches.
3. Deterministic integration and end-to-end scripts validate nominal, degraded, failure, and recovery scenarios.
4. Validation emits structured logs/artifacts with at least: `timestamp`, `component`, `scenario_id`, `correlation_id`, `decision_path`, `inputs`, `outcome`, `reason_code`/`error_code`, and `artifact_path`.
5. Release/promotion decisions are gated by auditable evidence bundles and explicit go/no-go criteria.","acceptance_criteria":"1. Integration tests prove that a demoted `v1` reflex is correctly superseded by a newly synthesized `v2` reflex.
2. Version lineage is durably recorded in the Evidence Ledger and the YAML metadata.
3. `v1` is prevented from executing once `v2` is promoted.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-23T00:14:32.962072Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-02-23T19:10:25.131408Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ft-tkpgy.4.4","depends_on_id":"ft-tkpgy.4","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-23T00:14:32.962072Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"ft-tkpgy.4.4","depends_on_id":"ft-tkpgy.4.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-23T00:14:32.962072Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

@@ -0,0 +1,53 @@
+#!/bin/bash
+set -e
+
+# Get mapping from title to ID
+br list --json | jq -r '.[] | "\(.id)|\(.title)"' > issues.tmp
+
+function get_id() {
+    local title="$1"
+    grep "$title" issues.tmp | head -n 1 | cut -d'|' -f1
+}
+
+# Epic 1
+EPIC1=$(get_id "Epic: Eradicate Panic Surfaces in Library Code")
+T1_1=$(get_id "Remove panic! macros from replay_capture.rs")
+T1_2=$(get_id "Replace Mutex::lock().unwrap() poison handling")
+T1_3=$(get_id "Audit and remove serde_json::from_str(...).unwrap()")
+if [ -n "$EPIC1" ]; then
+    [ -n "$T1_1" ] && br dep add $T1_1 $EPIC1
+    [ -n "$T1_2" ] && br dep add $T1_2 $EPIC1
+    [ -n "$T1_3" ] && br dep add $T1_3 $EPIC1
+fi
+
+# Epic 2
+EPIC2=$(get_id "Epic: Concurrency and Async Safety")
+T2_1=$(get_id "Eliminate async lock guards held across await points")
+T2_2=$(get_id "Fix unjoined std::thread::spawn calls")
+T2_3=$(get_id "Enforce TcpStream shutdown")
+if [ -n "$EPIC2" ]; then
+    [ -n "$T2_1" ] && br dep add $T2_1 $EPIC2
+    [ -n "$T2_2" ] && br dep add $T2_2 $EPIC2
+    [ -n "$T2_3" ] && br dep add $T2_3 $EPIC2
+fi
+
+# Epic 3
+EPIC3=$(get_id "Epic: Memory Safety and Legacy Cleanup")
+T3_1=$(get_id "Refactor mem::transmute in legacy teletypewriter and vtparse")
+T3_2=$(get_id "Remove mem::forget from session_restore.rs")
+if [ -n "$EPIC3" ]; then
+    [ -n "$T3_1" ] && br dep add $T3_1 $EPIC3
+    [ -n "$T3_2" ] && br dep add $T3_2 $EPIC3
+fi
+
+# Epic 4
+EPIC4=$(get_id "Epic: Robustness and Performance Hotspots")
+T4_1=$(get_id "Hoist Regex::new out of loops")
+T4_2=$(get_id "Guard division and modulo operations against zero")
+if [ -n "$EPIC4" ]; then
+    [ -n "$T4_1" ] && br dep add $T4_1 $EPIC4
+    [ -n "$T4_2" ] && br dep add $T4_2 $EPIC4
+fi
+
+br sync --flush-only
+rm issues.tmp
